name: Incremental Type Checking
summary: Dependency tracking at finer granularity than files: parsed file, module interface, symbol table, import graph, type of declaration, body check result, trait obligations, macro expansion result, diagnostics. Edits in public signatures invalidate dependents; edits in bodies may not invalidate interfaces; macro expansion may change scopes and types.

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
Incremental semantic analysis needs dependency tracking at a finer granularity than files. Useful keys include parsed file, module interface, symbol table, import graph, type of declaration, body check result, trait obligations, macro expansion result, and diagnostics.

The distinguishing axis across designs is *what level of granularity invalidates*: a whole module (coarse, simple), a single declaration (medium), a single inference fact (fine, sophisticated). rust-analyzer and Salsa target the fine end; rustc batch mode is at the coarse end.

sections.title: Challenges
